name: Hadza

ancestors.notes: Hadza is a language isolate (Glottolog hadz1240); the clade node represents its own one-member family.

notes: Language isolate of Tanzania, a click language. The documented practical (Miller-Anyawire) orthography deliberately writes the three clicks with the Latin letters c (dental), q (alveolar) and x (lateral) rather than the click letters, uses h-digraphs for aspiration and gemination (cc/qq/xx) for glottalized nasal clicks. Nasal vowels are written with a following n. This orthography is documented but not widely used by speakers and no single community standard exists, so this is a skeleton map. Phonemic vowel length, intervocalic glottal stop and pitch/stress are not encoded; the dl grapheme is a marginal lateral ejective given as a tentative value.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Hadza (hadz1240)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
